name: Wake Island (United States)

colors.symbolism: the lower field, recalling the bloodshed of the 1941 Battle of Wake Island
the hoist wedge, recalling the surrounding Pacific Ocean and the territory's American sovereignty
the central disc and three stars

description: A field divided diagonally between white above and red below, with a wedge-shaped blue panel along the hoist. A large golden disc is centred on the field with the words 'WAKE ISLANDS' arranged around it; three small golden five-pointed stars stand on the blue hoist wedge.
history: Wake Island is an unincorporated US territory administered by the US Air Force; it has no formally adopted flag. This unofficial flag, designed to evoke the atoll's three islets and its place in American Pacific history (notably the 1941 Battle of Wake Island), has been flown locally. The ISO 3166-3 code WKUM records the territory's former ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 code 'WK' (1974-1986), withdrawn when the atoll was merged into the broader 'UM' (United States Minor Outlying Islands) entry.

features.symbolism: the field is divided diagonally from upper-fly to lower-hoist: white above for the calm sky over the atoll, red below recalling the bloodshed of the 1941 Battle of Wake Island
the surrounding Pacific Ocean and the territory's American sovereignty
the sun rising over the Pacific

features.role: three golden stars on the hoist pile
features.symbolism: the three islets of the Wake atoll: Wake, Wilkes, and Peale

features.role: the legend 'WAKE ISLANDS' encircling the central disc, with letters spaced around the sun
